task_summary: SP36 retried the explicit compatibility-output cleanup after the user reported the gpt-5.5 quota reset. The run reached 13 work steps, but no product edits landed because the operator misread the new nested --report shape as step 0 and killed the process before retrying the remaining README usage assertion repair.

tests_run.result: operator killed the process after more than 15 minutes, but later report inspection showed the run had reached 13 nested .work_report steps

tests_run.command: jq '{generated_at, heartbeat_at, phase, work_report: {stop_reason: .work_report.stop_reason, steps_len: (.work_report.steps | length), last_step_index: .work_report.steps[-1].index, last_verified_state: .work_report.steps[-1].model_turn.action_plan.working_memory.last_verified_state}}' /tmp/mew-wisp-explicit-compat-output-retry-report.json
tests_run.result: generated_at 2026-04-30T06:36:52Z, phase running, .work_report.stop_reason max_steps, .work_report steps_len 13, last step index 13, verifier state: 51 passed with only README usage expectation failing after rollback
worktree clean after killed attempt; no product files changed

proof_artifacts: worktree remained clean; no product-code rescue edits were made
notes: This supersedes the initial first-turn-timeout diagnosis for the same task/session. The reusable implementation-lane finding is that the report/live status surface made active nested .work_report progress easy to miss; issue #33 was edited with the corrected debug details.
